summary: The diff shows a debugging and fix implementation where the backend was migrated to an empty actor state, requiring the frontend to adapt with type assertions, fallback handling, and comprehensive error logging. The changes primarily focus on graceful degradation when backend methods are unavailable, while maintaining UI functionality and adding user-facing alerts about backend unavailability.

missing_requirements.requirement: Investigate and identify why the application is not working in the deployed environment, checking for runtime errors, failed network requests, authentication issues, or broken component rendering
missing_requirements.reason: While logging was added throughout (App.tsx, useAuth.ts, useQueries.ts), there is no evidence of actual investigation results, root cause identification, or documentation of what specific issues were found in the deployed environment. The diff shows preventive error handling but not diagnostic investigation.

missing_requirements.requirement: Fix any identified issues preventing the application from functioning, including backend canister initialization errors, frontend build configuration problems, or authentication flow breakages
missing_requirements.reason: The backend was intentionally emptied (main.mo shows only migration with empty actor), meaning core features cannot be operational. Authentication flow calls non-existent backend methods (login/logout). This is a workaround with fallbacks, not a fix that restores functionality.

missing_requirements.requirement: Verify that the backend main.mo actor compiles without errors and all public methods are accessible from the frontend
missing_requirements.reason: The backend main.mo is intentionally empty (only contains migration to empty actor). No public methods exist for frontend access. Frontend code explicitly checks if methods exist and returns fallback values when they don't, confirming methods are NOT accessible.

hallucinated_features.description: Backend migration module with data structure definitions for StudySession, UserSettings, StreakData, and SyllabusChapter
hallucinated_features.reason: Migration module (backend/migration.mo) was not requested in the BuildRequest. The requirement was to debug and fix issues, not to create migration infrastructure. This appears to be preparation for future work rather than a requested debugging feature.

hallucinated_features.description: Local TypeScript type definitions file duplicating backend types
hallucinated_features.reason: Creating frontend/src/lib/types.ts with local type definitions was not requested. The BuildRequest explicitly stated to preserve existing data structures, not create duplicate type definitions in the frontend.

hallucinated_features.description: Backend availability alert component in the main App
hallucinated_features.reason: Adding a user-facing alert about backend unavailability was not requested. The requirement was to fix issues so the application works, not to add UI elements that inform users the backend is non-functional.

hallucinated_features.description: Method existence validation utility function in useQueries
hallucinated_features.reason: The checkMethod helper function that validates backend method existence was not requested. This is infrastructure for handling a non-functional backend rather than fixing the backend to make it functional.
